title: Central Point Police Department Flock Camera policy, invoice, map, and contracts

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the following records, that have already been requested to Central Point Police Department. By requesting previously produced records, I am hopeful that this request will require minimal labor to locate, review and produce the records. Please let me know if you need any additional information or clarification regarding the records I am seeking.

The records I am seeking are:

(1) all policies Central Point Police Department has that oversee and/or regulate the operation and use of Automated License Plate Readers, including the operation and use of Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares
(2) a copy of any and all invoices Central Point Police Department has between any and all Automated License Plate Readers softwares and programs, including Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares
(3) a map of all ALPR cameras that Central Point Police Department owns, operates or has access to
(4) a copy of any and all license and user agreements, contracts, memorandums of understanding or other similar type documents that describe and/or regulate the use, retention, collection, and sharing of ALPR data including but not limited to the agreements between Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares

Please segregate all non-exempt material, and “black out” rather than “white out” any exempt material. Please cite specific exemptions in every instance that  the public body claims an exemption.

Thank you so much!

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Southern Oregon Flock Off

title: Medford Police (OR) Flock "immigration" searches

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Medford Police (OR) Flock "immigration" searches
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of all audit logs, search logs, or usage reports related to your agency's use of Flock "ALPR" systems for the period of January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed, and which include any and all searches conducted for the listed reason:

-   Immigration

 This log should include but is not limited to:

-   Records of license plate searches performed by agency personnel
-   Timestamps of searches
-   User identification information (names, badge numbers, or user IDs)
-   Any associated case numbers or investigation references

Some of these records may be available as the "Organization Audit" and "Network Audit" in the Flock software's "Insights" section.

Please provide records in their original electronic format (CSV, Excel, or similar) where possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: CalGang (San Diego County Sheriff's Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

A memoradnum of understanding is required for outside agencies to access CalGang databases. I am requesting the MOUs between this department and ICE and Customs and Border Protection (CBP). I am requesting copies of those requests from 2016 to today. Along with the information below, for the same time frames. 

An Agency that is interested in accessing the CalGang database shall submit a written
request to the Department or the local Node Administrator on the Agency’s letterhead. The
written request shall provide the following:
(1) A reason for its need to access the CalGang database.
(A) The Agency shall demonstrate a need to know and a right to know to satisfy
subdivision (e)(1).
(2) The number of Users who will be accessing the CalGang database at that Agency and
the level of access that is requested for each User.
(3) The Agency’s assigned originating agency identifier (ORI) number, if applicable.
(4) The addresses of the facilities within the Agency where Users will access the
CalGang database.
(5) The name and contact information for the person(s) designated by the Agency as the
point of contact and training coordinator. If the Agency’s point of contact and training
coordinator are two different persons, the Agency shall identify both.
(6) If data entry access is requested, the Agency shall identify the classification(s),
rank(s), and related expertise of the person(s) who will be responsible for performing the
supervisory review required by sections 752.8 and 753.4; and describe the organizational
structure and process through which the supervisory review process required by sections
752.8 and 753.4 will occur.


Thank you

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: Law enforcement informal and formal settlement data and reports (Reno Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Materials related to and sufficient to show all instances of alleged or actual inappropriate or illegal behavior or actions resulting in compensation or expenditures involving this agency. 

Responsive materials include records of all settlements (formal and informal) and payments to which this agency was or is a party for the period from January 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed. Responsive materials include those for matters and legal claims resolved prior to the initiation of any litigation.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request the opportunity to inspect the following records. If these records exist in electronic format, I request that they be provided in their native or searchable electronic format (e.g., Excel, CSV, or searchable PDF) pursuant to Government Code § 7922.570, which shall serve as my inspection of the records. As citizen investigating state-level ADA compliance and grievance procedures, I am specifically interested in the volume, handling, and resolution of complaints submitted via the Caltrans online portal and the ada.compliance.office@dot.ca.gov email address.

Records Requested

Case Management Log/Database Export: A digital export of the tracking log or database used to manage ADA grievances. Based on recent test filings, I am aware that Caltrans issues sequential case numbers (e.g., Case #11963). I am requesting a list of all cases filed from January 1, 2022, to the present, including the following fields:

Case Number

Date Received

Subject/Nature of Complaint (e.g., "Inaccessible Curb Ramp," "Website Accessibility")

Location (District, County, Route, or Postmile if applicable)

Current Status (Open, Closed, Pending)

Date of Resolution/Closure

Photographs (if applicable) 


Aggregated Reporting: Any annual or quarterly summary reports generated by the ADA Compliance Office regarding the total number of grievances received, the average time to resolution, and the geographic distribution of complaints.

Privacy and Redaction (California Govt. Code § 7927.700)
In the interest of protecting individual privacy and expediting this request, I am NOT requesting any "Personally Identifiable Information" (PII). Please redact the following information from the responsive records:

Names of complainants.

Home addresses, personal phone numbers, or personal email addresses of complainants.

Specific medical information or descriptions of individual disabilities.

I am requesting only the substance of the grievance (the infrastructure or service failure) and the administrative handling of the file.

Production of Records
If you contend that any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, please provide a written response citing the specific statutory exemption and a brief explanation of how the exemption applies to the withheld information.

Thank you for the state of California's dedication to transparency, and to Caltran's dedication to fulfilling the promise of the Americans with Disabilities Act.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 6250 et seq.) ("CPRA”), and all other applicable laws, please disclose the following records.

Any and all autopsy, death investigation, and coroner’s reports relating to the death of:

Name: Alfredo Flores-Bravo, Date of birth: 04-04-1965, Date of death: 07-05-2011

If records pursuant to this request are possessed in machine-readable format such as an .xls, .csv spreadsheet file, please provide the record in this format. Please disclose these records in the format in which they are kept, e.g., .ppt, .pptx, .doc, or .docx.

The fundamental rule of the CPRA is a presumption of public access. “In other words, [A]ll public records are subject to disclosure unless the Legislature has expressly provided to the contrary.” Williams v. Superior Court, 5 Cal. 4th 337 (1993). This presumption finds further support in the California Constitution, as amended by Proposition 59 in 2004. “A statute, court rule, or other authority, including those in effect on the effective date of this subdivision, shall be broadly construed if it furthers the people's right of access, and narrowly construed if it limits the right of access.” Cal. Const. Art. 1 § 3(b)(2).

Please provide a determination on this request within 10 days of your receipt of it. If you determine that any or all of the information qualifies for an exemption from disclosure, please note whether, as is normally the case, the exemption is discretionary, and if so whether it is necessary in this case to exercise your discretion to withhold the information. If you determine that some but not all of the information is exempt from disclosure and that you intend to withhold it, please redact it for the time being and make the rest available as requested. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any or all of the information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

Please apply a fee waiver to this request. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please provide notification of any duplication costs exceeding $50 before you duplicate the records. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please further provide an index of all other requests since 2018 for which a fee waiver was denied, and the reason therefore.

Please respond to this request and disclose the records electronically by email to this MuckRock email address. Please let us know if you have any questions or if we can help ensure this request is the least burdensome possible for your agency while providing a correct and full disclosure in the public interest.


Thanks so much, 

Dillon Bergin
